user.description: Exploring AI, business, and internet trends. Sharing what I learn along the way
user.name: Manav

tweets.text:
A new open-source GitHub alternative launched this year with a familiar promise:

Your code. Your server. Your rules.

Then developers opened the repository and found more than fifteen separate CLAUDE.md files.

The project is called gitdot, a self-hosted git forge designed to https://t.co/orkYzVJPQN

tweets.text:

The headlines were technically true.

The full story was much more complicated.

On June 1, Shanghai-based MiniMax released M3, an open-weight model combining three capabilities that rarely https://t.co/OlnzPSsGtw

tweets.text:
Europe just launched what its backers call the first sovereign open-source alternative to Microsoft 365.

Within 24 hours, the people behind LibreOffice publicly called the claim misleading.

The project is Euro-Office, a coalition backed by Nextcloud, IONOS, Proton, and other https://t.co/1muuHhn1cS

tweets.text:
Three AI companies looked at the exact same business problem this year and made three completely opposite bets. One of them put it on a Super Bowl billboard.

OpenAI launched ads inside ChatGPT on February 9, 2026, for free and low-cost Go tier users in the United States. Product https://t.co/8EmmWzyRmx

tweets.text:
Every AI lab spent three years racing to build the smartest model. In 2026, the smartest people in the room stopped caring which model you use entirely.

tweets.name: Manav

tweets.text:
An open-weight model from a Chinese lab now resolves real production GitHub issues autonomously at a rate beating every open model published before it, and you can download the complete weights and run it yourself starting today.
